'''Petero Benjamin "Ben" Nakubuwai''' (born 15 March 1996) is a [[Fiji national rugby league team|Fiji]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a prop for the [[Norths Devils]] in the [[Queensland Cup]].
 
Nakubuwai previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]] in the [[National Rugby League]] and the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]], and was a member of Fiji's squad for the [[2017 Rugby League World Cup|2017 World Cup]].

==Background==
Nakubuwai was born in [[Suva ]], Fiji to [[Fijian people|Fijian]] parents [[Pio Nakubuwai|Pio]] and Eseta.<ref name=phantoms>{{cite web|date=17 June 2008|title=Rep Honours for Big Ben|url=http://www.phantomsjunior.rugbynet.com.au/default.asp?id=131404|publisher=Leeton Phantoms Junior Rugby Club|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20080719050725/http://www.phantomsjunior.rugbynet.com.au/default.asp?id=131404|archive-date=19 July 2008}}</ref> His father was a [[dual-code rugby international]] for Fiji during the 1990s, and is known as one of the pioneers of [[rugby league in Fiji]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Cabenatabua|first=Leone|date=2 March 2014|title=Inducted|url=http://fijisun.com.fj/2014/03/02/inducted/|publisher=Fiji Sun|access-date=14 February 2018}}</ref>

Nakubuwai grew up playing [[rugby union]] from the age of eight for the Leeton Phantoms,<ref name=phantoms /> and later took up rugby league at a junior level at age fourteen, playing for the Yanco-Wamoon Hawks and Bidgee Hurricanes.<ref>{{Cite web|title=Player profile|url=https://www.fijitimes.com/player-profile-46/|access-date=2021-05-20|website=FijiTimes|language=en}}</ref> Having been spotted by the [[Melbourne Storm]], Nakubwai moved to Melbourne after completing his [[Higher School Certificate (New South Wales)|Higher School Certificate]] to play in their [[S. G. Ball Cup]] team.<ref>{{cite web|last=Gray|first=John|date=1 October 2013|title=Storm front - Hurricanes young gun on Melbourne's radar|url=http://www.irrigator.com.au/story/1811082/storm-front-hurricanes-young-gun-on-melbournes-radar/|publisher=Irrigator|access-date=20 August 2017}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Early career===
Nakubuwai joined Melbourne's [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|National Youth Competition]] team in 2015, playing 42 matches over two seasons.<ref>{{cite web|title=N|url=https://nycdatabase.org/players/n/|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150624123605/http://nycdatabase.org/players/n/|url-status=usurped|archive-date=24 June 2015|publisher=NYC Database}}</ref> He made his international debut for Fiji against [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]] in May 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://matchcentre.nrl.com/match/2016/133/20161330130/#team-lists|title=NRL Match Centre 2017|website=matchcentre.nrl.com|access-date=20 August 2017}}</ref> In December 2016, Nakubuwai signed with the [[Gold Coast Titans]].<ref>{{cite web|last=Webeck|first=Tony|date=23 December 2016|title=Titans snare 'next Petero' from Storm|url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2016/12/23/titans-snare-next-petero-from-storm/|publisher=NRL.com|access-date=14 February 2018}}</ref>

===2017===
Nakubuwai spent most of the 2017 season playing for one of the Titans' feeder clubs, the [[Tweed Heads Seagulls]], in the [[Queensland Cup]]. He was called upon to play two NRL matches for the Titans at the end of the season.<ref>{{cite web|date=18 August 2017|title=Nakubuwai reflects on Titans debut|url=https://www.qrl.com.au/news/2017/08/18/nakubuwai-reflects-on-titans-debut/|publisher=QRL.com.au|access-date=14 February 2018}}</ref>

On 29 November 2017, it was revealed Nakubuwai had signed a two-year deal to join English side [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/nrl-market-watch-panthers-code-switch-consideration-knights-contract-twist/news-story/45ad45917a5cd394331f28fc77110b53|title=NRL Market Watch: Panther's code switch consideration, Knights contract twist|publisher=Fox Sports Australia|date=29 November 2017|access-date=24 January 2018}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|date=1 December 2017|title=Ben Nakubuwai: Salford Red Devils sign Fiji international|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/42194542|publisher=BBC Sport|access-date=14 February 2018}}</ref>

===2022===
On 26 April 2022 it was announced that Nakubuwai had signed for [[Leigh Centurions|Leigh]] until the end of the season. On 28 May, he played for Leigh in their [[2022 RFL 1895 Cup|1895 Cup]] final victory over [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]],<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkshirepost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/featherstone-rovers-1895-cup-final-defeat-to-leigh-centurions-gives-brian-mcdermotts-side-signal-of-their-progress-3713313/|title=Featherstone Rovers: 1895 Cup final defeat to Leigh Centurions gives Brian McDermott's side signal of their progress|website=Yorkshire Post}}</ref> and on 2 October appeared off the bench in the [[2022 RFL Championship| Championship]] grand final victory against [[Batley Bulldogs]].<ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/63108748 | title=Championship play-off final: Leigh 44-12 Batley Bulldogs - Adrian Lam's Centurions march back to Super League | date=2 October 2022 | access-date=14 March 2025 | publisher=BBC Sport}}</ref>

===2023===
On 12 August 2023, Nakubuwai played in the [[2023 Challenge Cup final]] victory over [[Hull Kingston Rovers]]. It was Leigh's first major trophy in 52 years.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/66463438 | title=Challenge Cup final: Hull KR 16-17 Leigh Leopards - Leigh claim first Wembley final win in 52 years | website=BBC Sport | date=12 August 2023 | access-date=14 March 2025}}</ref> He played 19 games for Leigh in the [[2023 Super League season]] as the club finished fifth on the table and qualified for the playoffs. He played in their elimination playoff loss against Hull Kingston Rovers.<ref>{{cite web | url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/leigh-leopards-2023-in-review/#:~:text=Overall%20playing%20record%3A,For%20694%2C%20Against%20580./ | title=Leigh Leopards 2023 season in review | website=Leigh Leopards}}</ref>

===2024===
On 1 Jun 2024 Nakubuwai signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[2024 RFL Championship season|Championship]] on a season-long loan,<ref>{{cite web | website=Leigh Leopards | date=1 Jun 2024 | access-date=4 Jun 2024 | title=BEN NAKUBUWAI JOINS FEATHERSTONE ROVERS ON LOAN | url=https://leighleopards.co.uk/ben-nakubuwai-joins-featherstone-rovers-on-loan/}}</ref> helping Rovers to sixth-place and playoff qualification.

===2025===
On 14 March 2025 it was reported that Ben had departed [[Leigh Leopards]].<ref>{{cite web | website=Love Rugby League | author=Aaron Bower|date=14 Mar 2025 | title=Leigh handed quota opportunity as forward exits with Nene Macdonald link addressed | url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leigh-handed-quota-opportunity-as-forward-exits-with-nene-macdonald-link-addressed}}</ref><ref>{{cite news | url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/crew0j514qzo | title=Fiji forward Nakubuwai leaves Leigh | publisher=BBC Sport | date=14 March 2025 | access-date=14 March 2025}}</ref>

On 2 April 2025 it was reported that he had re-joined the [[Queensland Cup]] team [[Norths Devils]], linking up again with [[Rohan Smith (rugby league)|Rohan Smith]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=2 Apr 2025|author=Aaron Bower|title=Released Leigh Leopards man lands new club in Australia|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/released-leigh-leopards-man-lands-new-club-in-australia}}</ref>
